GigaCode
  • API Reference
  • View page source

API Reference

Complete API documentation for GigaCode modules and tools.

Core Tool

Agent-facing tool interface for GPU-accelerated code embedding.

Chunks code at AST boundaries, keeps a persistent FAISS index in GPU memory when available, and exposes a read-write-commit workflow.

class gigacode.gigacode_tool.CodeEmbeddingTool(work_dir: str | Path, model_name: str | None = None, device: str | None = None, threshold_mb: float = 500.0, use_gpu: bool = True, gpu_id: int = 0, max_buffers: int = 10, enable_prometheus: bool = False, prometheus_port: int = 9090)[source]

Bases: PhasesIntegrationMixin

Embed a codebase into GPU/CPU buffers and expose search + cluster.

Parameters:

  • work_dir – Directory where buffer files and metadata are persisted.

  • model_name – Sentence-transformers model name.

  • device – torch device ("cuda", "cpu", or None for auto).

  • threshold_mb – Size-guard threshold in megabytes.

  • use_gpu – Whether to mirror the FAISS index to GPU when possible.

  • max_buffers – Maximum number of in-memory indices to keep (LRU eviction).

  • enable_prometheus – Enable Prometheus metrics export (default False).

  • prometheus_port – Port for Prometheus metrics endpoint (default 9090).

Initialize CodeEmbeddingTool.

Parameters:

  • work_dir – Working directory for embeddings and caches.

  • model_name – Sentence transformer model name (default: ‘all-MiniLM-L6-v2’).

  • device – PyTorch device (‘cpu’, ‘cuda’, ‘cuda:0’, etc.).

  • threshold_mb – Codebase size threshold (default 500 MB).

  • use_gpu – Enable GPU FAISS index mirroring (default True).

  • gpu_id – GPU device ID for FAISS (default 0). Only used if use_gpu=True.

  • max_buffers – Max embedded codebases in memory (LRU limit, default 10).

  • enable_prometheus – Enable Prometheus metrics export via HTTP (default False).

  • prometheus_port – Port for /metrics endpoint (default 9090).

__init__(work_dir: str | Path, model_name: str | None = None, device: str | None = None, threshold_mb: float = 500.0, use_gpu: bool = True, gpu_id: int = 0, max_buffers: int = 10, enable_prometheus: bool = False, prometheus_port: int = 9090) → None[source]

Initialize CodeEmbeddingTool.

Parameters:

  • work_dir – Working directory for embeddings and caches.

  • model_name – Sentence transformer model name (default: ‘all-MiniLM-L6-v2’).

  • device – PyTorch device (‘cpu’, ‘cuda’, ‘cuda:0’, etc.).

  • threshold_mb – Codebase size threshold (default 500 MB).

  • use_gpu – Enable GPU FAISS index mirroring (default True).

  • gpu_id – GPU device ID for FAISS (default 0). Only used if use_gpu=True.

  • max_buffers – Max embedded codebases in memory (LRU limit, default 10).

  • enable_prometheus – Enable Prometheus metrics export via HTTP (default False).

  • prometheus_port – Port for /metrics endpoint (default 9090).

static get_tool_schemas() → list[dict[str, Any]][source]
static validate_schemas() → dict[str, Any][source]

Validate that hardcoded schemas match the actual CodeEmbeddingTool code.

Returns a dict with: - “valid”: bool indicating if all schemas are valid - “issues”: dict mapping method name to list of validation issues - “report”: human-readable validation report - “generated_count”: number of schemas generated - “validated_count”: number of schemas validated

Used to detect schema drift and ensure tool_schema.py stays in sync with implementation.

check_codebase(path: str | Path, pattern: str = '*.py') → dict[str, Any][source]
embed_codebase(path: str | Path, language_hint: str | None = None, pattern: str = '*.py', sliding_window_size: int = 30) → dict[str, Any][source]

Embed a codebase: chunk all files, embed chunks, build index.

Returns: EmbedResponse as dict with buffer_id, chunk_count, and status.

reload_codebase(buffer_id: str) → dict[str, Any][source]

Reload codebase from disk, detecting external changes.

semantic_search(buffer_id: str, query: str, top_k: int = 5, offset: int = 0) → dict[str, Any][source]

Semantic search via embeddings.

Delegates to SearchService when available (Phase 5), otherwise uses monolithic implementation.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer ID to search

  • query – Search query

  • top_k – Number of top results to return

  • offset – Pagination offset

Returns:

Dict with search results or error

semantic_search_streaming(buffer_id: str, query: str, top_k: int = 10, disclosure: str = 'signatures') → dict[str, Any][source]

Search with progressive result disclosure to save tokens.

Three disclosure levels: - “signatures”: Only function/class signatures (~8 tokens/match). - “details”: Signatures + docstrings + first 5 lines (~45 tokens/match). - “full”: Complete chunk text (~200 tokens/match).

Use expand_match() to incrementally expand a single match.

Expected savings: 84% for signatures-only vs full chunks.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • query – Search query string.

  • top_k – Number of top results.

  • disclosure – “signatures” | “details” | “full”.

Returns:

Dict with matches at the requested disclosure level, plus expandable flag and match_count.

expand_match(buffer_id: str, match_id: int, level: str = 'details') → dict[str, Any][source]

Expand a search match to a higher disclosure level.

Progressively reveals more detail for a single match without re-embedding or re-searching.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • match_id – Chunk index from a prior streaming search result.

  • level – “details” (signatures + docstring + first 5 lines) or “full” (complete text).

Returns:

Dict with expanded match data.

find_similar_intents(buffer_id: str, query: str, top_k: int = 3) → dict[str, Any][source]

Find cached intent clusters semantically similar to a query.

Helps when you’re not sure how to phrase a query — find related intents that have been cached previously.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • query – Query string to find similar intents for.

  • top_k – Maximum number of similar intents to return.

Returns:

Dict with similar_intents list, each containing canonical_query, similarity score, hit count, and most recent cached result.

get_intent_cache_stats(buffer_id: str) → dict[str, Any][source]

Get statistics for the intent cache.

Parameters:

buffer_id – Buffer handle.

Returns:

Dict with hits, misses, hit_rate, cluster_count, avg_similarity.

faceted_search(buffer_id: str, query: str, filters: dict[str, Any] | None = None, top_k: int = 10, include_uncertain: bool = True, confidence_threshold: float = 0.5) → dict[str, Any][source]

Perform faceted semantic search with filters and result explanation.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • query – Search query string.

  • filters – Optional filter dict with keys: - language (str): e.g. “python”, “javascript” - path_regex (str): regex to match file paths - type_in (list[str]): chunk types [“function”, “class”, …] - min_lines (int): minimum chunk line count - max_lines (int): maximum chunk line count - file_pattern (str): glob pattern e.g. “src/**/*.py”

  • top_k – Number of top results.

  • include_uncertain – Include matches with lower confidence.

  • confidence_threshold – Minimum score for confident matches.

Returns:

Dict with matches (each has score, confidence, score_breakdown, why), uncertain_matches, total_matches, filtered_out.

hybrid_search(buffer_id: str, query: str, top_k: int = 5, offset: int = 0, semantic_weight: float = 1.0, lexical_weight: float = 1.0) → dict[str, Any][source]

Combine FAISS semantic search with BM25 lexical search via RRF.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • query – Natural language or keyword query.

  • top_k – Number of results to return.

  • offset – Pagination offset.

  • semantic_weight – Weight for semantic rank contribution.

  • lexical_weight – Weight for lexical rank contribution.

Returns:

Dict with status and matches.

search_for(buffer_id: str, query: str, case_sensitive: bool = False, max_results: int = 50) → dict[str, Any][source]

Find every occurrence of query in the buffered source code.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • query – Substring to search for.

  • case_sensitive – If True, match case exactly.

  • max_results – Cap on the number of matches returned.

Returns:

Dict with status, matches (list of file/line/content), and total.

look_for_file(buffer_id: str, file_name: str) → dict[str, Any][source]

Find the location of a file within an embedded buffer.

Tries exact match, then basename match, then partial substring match. Returns the relative file path (within the buffer root) and the absolute path on disk.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • file_name – File name or path fragment to look for.

Returns:

Dict with status, file_location, absolute_path, match_type, and optionally candidates.

search_symbols(buffer_id: str, query: str, top_k: int = 10) → dict[str, Any][source]

Find variables, functions, classes, and methods matching query.

Performs two searches in parallel: 1. Name match — chunks whose name contains the query string (score=1.0). 2. Semantic match — top-K chunks by embedding similarity, filtered to

symbol types (function, class, method, struct, trait, enum, interface).

Results are deduplicated and merged (name matches rank first).

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • query – Word or phrase to look for.

  • top_k – Maximum number of symbol matches to return.

Returns:

SearchResponse with matches (list of SearchMatch objects with type, name, score).

search_by_type(buffer_id: str, type_pattern: str, top_k: int = 10) → dict[str, Any][source]

Find functions and classes by type signature pattern.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • type_pattern – Type pattern to search (e.g., “Callable[[str], bool]”, “dict[str, int]”).

  • top_k – Maximum results.

Returns:

Dict with matches, each containing type_signature, match_score, match_reason.

find_implementations(buffer_id: str, interface_name: str, top_k: int = 10) → dict[str, Any][source]

Find classes implementing a given interface/protocol.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • interface_name – Interface name (e.g., “DataStore”, “Iterator”).

  • top_k – Maximum results.

Returns:

Dict with implementations list, each with class_name, inheritance_type, confidence.

symbol_search(buffer_id: str, query: str) → dict[str, Any][source]

Search for symbols by name (exact, prefix, fuzzy).

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • query – Symbol name to search (e.g., “UserRepository”, “validate_email”).

Returns:

Dict with exact_matches, prefix_matches, fuzzy_matches.

get_symbol_definition(buffer_id: str, symbol: str) → dict[str, Any][source]

Jump to definition of a symbol.

Supports qualified names like “UserRepository.validate_email”.

get_symbol_references(buffer_id: str, symbol: str, top_k: int = 50) → dict[str, Any][source]

Find all references to a symbol across the codebase.

Returns reference locations with context lines and confidence scores.

list_file_symbols(buffer_id: str, file: str) → dict[str, Any][source]

List all symbols defined in a specific file.

cluster_code(buffer_id: str, threshold: float = 0.75) → dict[str, Any][source]

Find groups of semantically similar chunks within a buffer.

Uses a greedy clustering algorithm: 1. For each chunk, find all subsequent chunks (look-ahead window of 64)

with similarity > threshold

  1. Group them into a cluster

  2. Return clusters with ≥2 members, sorted by file/line

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer to cluster.

  • threshold – Cosine similarity threshold (0.0-1.0). Default 0.75. Increase for tighter clusters, decrease for looser.

Returns:

  • file: Relative path

  • start_line: First chunk’s start_line

  • end_line: Last chunk’s end_line

  • size: Number of chunks in cluster

  • avg_score: Average pairwise similarity within cluster

Return type:

Dict with status=”ok” and clusters list. Each cluster contains

Example

```python result = tool.cluster_code(“buffer_id”, threshold=0.8) for cluster in result[“clusters”]:

print(f”{cluster[‘file’]} ({cluster[‘size’]} chunks, “

f”avg_score={cluster[‘avg_score’]:.3f})”)

```

find_duplicates(buffer_id: str, threshold: float = 0.85) → dict[str, Any][source]

Find near-duplicate code chunks within a buffer.

Detects code snippets with high semantic similarity (≥ threshold). Useful for: - Finding copy-paste code - Identifying dead code (after refactoring) - Planning refactoring efforts (consolidate duplicates)

Algorithm: Uses embedding similarity computed during indexing. Delegates to duplicate_detector.find_duplicates().

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer to analyze.

  • threshold – Cosine similarity threshold (0.0-1.0). Default 0.85. Increase for stricter matching; decrease to catch loose dups.

Returns:

  • file1, start_line1, end_line1 — First chunk location

  • file2, start_line2, end_line2 — Second chunk location

  • similarity: Cosine similarity score

Return type:

Dict with status=”ok” and duplicates list. Each entry contains

Example

```python result = tool.find_duplicates(“buffer_id”, threshold=0.9) for dup in result[“duplicates”]:

print(f”Dup: {dup[‘file1’]}:{dup[‘start_line1’]} ≈ “

f”{dup[‘file2’]}:{dup[‘start_line2’]} ” f”(similarity={dup[‘similarity’]:.3f})”)

```

pack_context(buffer_id: str, query: str, max_tokens: int = 8192, top_k: int = 20) → dict[str, Any][source]

Return an optimally packed set of chunks fitting within max_tokens.

Uses hybrid search to find the most relevant chunks, then greedily packs them by score until the token budget is exhausted.

pack_context_smart(buffer_id: str, query: str, max_tokens: int = 8192, top_k: int = 20, deduplicate: bool = True, strip_boilerplate: bool = True, strip_docstrings: str = 'auto', exclude_types: list[str] | None = None, exclude_test_files: bool = False, min_lines: int = 3, max_lines: int = 200, granularity: str = 'smart') → dict[str, Any][source]

Smart context packing with token-saving optimizations.

Applies multiple filters to reduce token usage while maintaining relevance: 1. Deduplication: remove copy-pasted chunks 2. Boilerplate stripping: remove imports, licenses, __all__ 3. Docstring stripping: remove verbose docstrings (unless query

asks for docs)

  1. Type filtering: skip orphans, sliding windows, tests

  2. Size filtering: skip tiny or huge chunks

  3. Granularity: use signatures-only for low-relevance chunks

Expected token savings: 30-40% on typical codebases.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • query – Search query for finding relevant chunks.

  • max_tokens – Maximum tokens for assembled context.

  • top_k – Number of search results to consider.

  • deduplicate – Remove near-duplicate chunks (default True).

  • strip_boilerplate – Remove import/license headers (default True).

  • strip_docstrings – “auto” | “always” | “never”.

  • exclude_types – Chunk types to skip (default [“orphan”, “sliding”]).

  • exclude_test_files – Skip test files (default False).

  • min_lines – Minimum chunk line count (default 3).

  • max_lines – Maximum chunk line count (default 200).

  • granularity – “signatures” | “bodies” | “smart” (default “smart”).

Returns:

Dict with packed_chunks, total_tokens, savings stats, and filter report.

pack_context_hierarchical(buffer_id: str, query: str, max_tokens: int = 8192, levels: list[str] | None = None, top_k_files: int = 5, top_k_chunks: int = 10) → dict[str, Any][source]

Pack context hierarchically for LLM consumption.

Provides three levels of detail: 1. File summaries (overview, top-level definitions) 2. Chunk summaries (function/class signatures + docstrings) 3. Specific lines (query-relevant line ranges)

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • query – Search query.

  • max_tokens – Maximum tokens for assembled context.

  • levels – Which levels to include [“file_summary”, “chunk”, “lines”].

  • top_k_files – Number of top files to include.

  • top_k_chunks – Number of top chunks per file.

Returns:

Dict with hierarchy list and total_tokens.

related_code(buffer_id: str, file: str, start_line: int, end_line: int | None = None, include: list[str] | None = None, top_k: int = 10, max_tokens: int = 8192) → dict[str, Any][source]

Assemble cross-file related context for a code location.

Given a file and line range, finds: - Callers of the target symbol - Related test files - Interface definitions (classes, traits, protocols) - Semantic neighbors (similar chunks in other files) - Imports for the target chunk

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • file – Relative file path within the buffer.

  • start_line – Start line (1-based).

  • end_line – End line (1-based). If None, uses start_line.

  • include – Context types to include: [“callers”, “tests”, “interfaces”, “imports”, “semantic”] Default: all.

  • top_k – Maximum number of results per category.

  • max_tokens – Token budget for assembled context.

Returns:

Dict with status, the assembled context fields (callers, tests, interfaces, imports, semantic_neighbors), and total_tokens.

get_context(buffer_id: str, file: str, start_line: int, end_line: int | None = None, include: list[str] | None = None, top_k: int = 10, max_tokens: int = 8192) → dict[str, Any][source]

Get cross-file context for a code location (alias for related_code).

Returns assembled context with callers, tests, interfaces, imports, and semantic neighbors, token-budgeted to max_tokens.

refactor_rename(buffer_id: str, old_name: str, new_name: str, scope: str = 'buffer', dry_run: bool = True) → dict[str, Any][source]

Rename a symbol across the codebase.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • old_name – Current symbol name.

  • new_name – Desired symbol name.

  • scope – “buffer” to rename across all files.

  • dry_run – If True, returns preview without modifying.

Returns:

Dict with status, changed_files, changes list.

edit_symbol(buffer_id: str, symbol_name: str, new_body: str, language_hint: str | None = None, dry_run: bool = True) → dict[str, Any][source]

Replace the body of a symbol while preserving its signature.

Finds the symbol by name, replaces its body with new_body, and leaves the signature (def name(…):) intact.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • symbol_name – Symbol to edit (e.g. “fetch_data”).

  • new_body – New body text (lines will be indented to match original).

  • language_hint – Language for parsing heuristics.

  • dry_run – If True, returns preview without modifying.

Returns:

Dict with status, change preview, and applied info.

add_parameter(buffer_id: str, symbol_name: str, param: str, default: str | None = None, language_hint: str | None = None, dry_run: bool = True) → dict[str, Any][source]

Add a parameter to a function signature.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • symbol_name – Function to modify.

  • param – Parameter name.

  • default – Default value string (e.g. “None”, “[]”).

  • language_hint – Language for parsing heuristics.

  • dry_run – If True, returns preview without modifying.

Returns:

Dict with status, change preview, and applied info.

extract_method(buffer_id: str, file: str, start_line: int, end_line: int, new_name: str, language_hint: str | None = None, dry_run: bool = True) → dict[str, Any][source]

Extract a range of lines into a new method.

Replaces the selected lines with a call to the new method and inserts the new method definition nearby.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • file – File containing the lines to extract.

  • start_line – First line to extract (1-based).

  • end_line – Last line to extract (1-based, inclusive).

  • new_name – Name for the extracted method.

  • language_hint – Language for parsing heuristics.

  • dry_run – If True, returns preview without modifying.

Returns:

Dict with status, preview, and applied info.

add_import(buffer_id: str, file: str, module: str, symbols: list[str] | None = None, language_hint: str | None = None, dry_run: bool = True) → dict[str, Any][source]

Add an import statement to a file.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • file – Target file path.

  • module – Module to import.

  • symbols – Specific symbols, or None for module import.

  • language_hint – Language for syntax selection.

  • dry_run – If True, returns preview without modifying.

Returns:

Dict with status, changed_files, changes list.

remove_import(buffer_id: str, file: str, module: str, language_hint: str | None = None, dry_run: bool = True) → dict[str, Any][source]

Remove an import statement from a file.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • file – Target file path.

  • module – Module to remove.

  • language_hint – Language for syntax selection.

  • dry_run – If True, returns preview without modifying.

Returns:

Dict with status, changed_files, changes list.

apply_patch(buffer_id: str, patch_text: str, dry_run: bool = True) → dict[str, Any][source]

Apply a unified diff patch to the buffer.

Accepts standard unified diff format (like git diff output). Supports multiple files in one patch.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • patch_text – Unified diff string with @@ hunk headers.

  • dry_run – If True, returns preview without modifying.

Returns:

Dict with changed_files, changes list, and status.

Example patch_text:

--- a/auth.py
+++ b/auth.py
@@ -45,7 +45,7 @@
 def validate_token(token: str) -> dict:
-    payload = jwt.decode(token, SECRET)
+    payload = jwt.decode(token, SECRET, options={"verify_exp": True})
     return payload
read_code(buffer_id: str, file: str | None = None, start_line: int = 1, end_line: int | None = None) → dict[str, Any][source]

Read file contents from buffer (delegates to BufferManager).

write_code(buffer_id: str, file: str, start_line: int | str, new_lines: list[str] | None = None, end_line: int | None = None) → dict[str, Any][source]
diff(buffer_id: str, file: str | None = None) → dict[str, Any][source]

Show diff between buffer and disk versions.

Delegates to BufferManager.diff for actual diff computation, then adapts the response for backward compatibility.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • file – Optional specific file to diff. If None, diffs all files.

Returns:

Dict with status, diffs (per-file diff details), and has_conflicts flag. For backward compatibility, also includes changed_files list.

discard(buffer_id: str, file: str | None = None) → dict[str, Any][source]
commit(buffer_id: str, dry_run: bool = False, check_impact: bool = True, force: bool = False) → dict[str, Any][source]

Commit buffer changes to disk with 3-way merge conflict handling and crash recovery.

Phase 3: Uses SnapshotManager for merge conflicts. Phase 4: Wraps with StateManager transactions for crash recovery. Phase 4b: Optional dependency-impact pre-check warns on high blast-radius.

  • If both buffer and disk modified: returns “conflict” status instead of aborting

  • Rebuilds embeddings before writing

  • Updates snapshot manifest after successful writes

  • Returns detailed conflict information for user resolution

  • Uses write-ahead logging (WAL) for crash recovery

  • If check_impact=True and risk is HIGH, blocks commit unless force=True

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer to commit

  • dry_run – If True, check what would be written without modifying files

  • check_impact – If True, run dependency risk analysis before committing.

  • force – If True, proceed with commit even if impact analysis reports HIGH risk.

Returns:

  • “status”: “ok”, “conflict”, “error”, or “blocked”

  • ”written_files”: List of successfully written files

  • ”conflict_files”: List of dicts with conflict details (file, message, line counts)

  • ”dry_run”: Whether this was a dry run

  • ”transaction_id”: Transaction ID (for debugging)

  • ”impact_analysis”: Included when check_impact=True and commit is blocked

Return type:

Dict with

run_impacted_tests(buffer_id: str, top_k: int = 20, timeout: int = 120, extra_args: list[str] | None = None) → dict[str, Any][source]

Find and run tests impacted by uncommitted (dirty) changes.

  1. Scans dirty files in the buffer.

  2. Extracts modified symbols from diffs.

  3. Finds test chunks that reference those files / symbols.

  4. Runs pytest on only the impacted test files (fast).

  5. Returns structured pass/fail with traces and token counts.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • top_k – Max number of impacted test files to run.

  • timeout – Max seconds for pytest execution.

  • extra_args – Additional pytest arguments.

Returns:

  • status: “ok” | “no_tests” | “skipped” | “error”

  • passed, failed, skipped, total

  • tests: list of individual test results

  • impacted_files: list of test files that were run

  • token_estimate: approximate tokens in the output

  • stdout, stderr (truncated)

Return type:

Dict with

analyze_impact(buffer_id: str, modified_symbols: list[str] | None = None, max_depth: int = 6) → dict[str, Any][source]

Analyze blast-radius of uncommitted (dirty) changes.

For each dirty file and modified symbol, walks upstream callers and incoming file dependencies to compute a risk score.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • modified_symbols – Optional list of symbol names that were changed. If None, symbols are inferred from diffs of dirty files.

  • max_depth – Max call-chain depth to traverse upward.

Returns:

  • status: “ok” | “error”

  • risk_level: “low” | “medium” | “high”

  • risk_score: 0.0 – 1.0

  • total_impacted_files: count

  • max_call_depth: deepest call chain found

  • test_coverage_present: bool

  • symbol_impacts: per-symbol caller lists and depths

  • file_impacts: per-file incoming deps and critical flags

  • recommendations: human-readable action items

  • message: one-line summary

Return type:

Dict with

execute_in_context(buffer_id: str, code: str, language: str = 'python', timeout: int = 30) → dict[str, Any][source]

Run arbitrary code against the codebase in a restricted sandbox.

The buffer root is injected into sys.path so the code can import modules from the codebase. Output is captured and returned; file I/O and dangerous imports are blocked by AST scanning.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • code – Source code string.

  • language – “python” or “javascript”.

  • timeout – Max seconds before SIGKILL.

Returns:

  • status: “ok” | “error” | “timeout” | “security_violation”

  • returncode: process exit code

  • stdout, stderr: captured output (truncated at 64KB)

  • execution_time_sec: wall-clock time

  • violations: list of security policy violations (if any)

  • truncated: bool

Return type:

Dict with

list_buffers() → dict[str, Any][source]

List all registered buffers.

delete_buffer(buffer_id: str) → dict[str, Any][source]

Delete a buffer.

create_alias(alias: str, buffer_id: str) → dict[str, Any][source]

Create a human-readable alias for a buffer ID.

Example

tool.create_alias(“main-project”, “uuid-here”) # Later: tool.semantic_search(“main-project”, query=”…”)

resolve_alias(alias: str) → dict[str, Any][source]

Resolve an alias to its buffer ID.

list_aliases() → dict[str, Any][source]

List all buffer aliases.

create_virtual_buffer(alias: str, buffer_ids: list[str], description: str = '') → dict[str, Any][source]

Create a virtual buffer aggregating multiple sub-buffers.

Example

tool.create_virtual_buffer(

“monorepo”, [“buf-frontend”, “buf-backend”, “buf-shared”], description=”Full monorepo view”

)

list_virtual_buffers() → dict[str, Any][source]

List all virtual buffers.

delete_virtual_buffer(alias: str) → dict[str, Any][source]

Delete a virtual buffer.

auto_resume(path: str | Path, pattern: str = '*.py') → dict[str, Any][source]

Check if a codebase was previously embedded and is unchanged.

Returns:

“resumed”, “buffer_id”: …, “num_chunks”: …} if found {“status”: “not_found”, “message”: “…”} if not

Return type:

{“status”

save_session(alias: str, buffer_ids: list[str] | None = None) → dict[str, Any][source]

Save current session state under an alias.

Parameters:

  • alias – Session name (e.g., “main-project”)

  • buffer_ids – Specific buffers to save. If None, saves all registered buffers.

load_session(alias: str) → dict[str, Any][source]

Restore a previously saved session.

Returns:

“ok”, “buffer_ids”: […], “missing”: […]}

Return type:

{“status”

list_sessions() → dict[str, Any][source]

List all saved sessions.

git_status(buffer_id: str) → dict[str, Any][source]

Get git working tree status for a buffer’s source directory.

Returns branch, ahead/behind counts, modified/staged/untracked files.

git_diff(buffer_id: str, file: str | None = None, against: str = 'HEAD') → dict[str, Any][source]

Get diff of file(s) against a git reference.

Parameters:

  • file – Specific file, or None for all.

  • against – “HEAD”, “STAGED”, commit hash, or branch name.

git_blame(buffer_id: str, file: str, line: int | None = None) → dict[str, Any][source]

Show who last modified each line of a file.

Parameters:

  • file – File path.

  • line – Specific line (1-based), or None for full file.

git_show(buffer_id: str, file: str, commit: str = 'HEAD') → dict[str, Any][source]

Show file content at a specific commit.

Parameters:

  • file – File path.

  • commit – Commit hash or reference.

check_buffer_state(buffer_id: str) → dict[str, Any][source]

Return the current state of a buffer (READY, DIRTY, REBUILDING).

Parameters:

buffer_id – Buffer handle.

Returns:

Dict with status, buffer_id, state, and optional state_changed_at timestamp.

estimate_budget(path: str | Path, pattern: str = '*.py') → dict[str, Any][source]

Estimate resource requirements before embedding a codebase.

Returns:

Dict with estimated_ram_mb, estimated_embed_time_s, estimated_search_latency_ms, num_files, num_lines, estimated_chunks, recommended_device, warnings.

get_memory_usage() → dict[str, Any][source]

Get current memory usage of the tool.

score_result_confidence(score: float, all_scores: list[float]) → dict[str, Any][source]

Score the confidence of a search result.

Parameters:

  • score – The result’s score.

  • all_scores – All scores from the same search.

Returns:

Dict with confidence (high/medium/low) and explanation.

get_cache_stats() → dict[str, Any][source]

Return cache utilization statistics (delegated to IndexManager).

health_check() → dict[str, Any][source]

Perform a health check and return system status (delegated to IndexManager).

static get_metrics() → dict[str, Any][source]

Export current metrics snapshot.

Returns a dict with counters (operation counts), gauges, histograms (latency and size distributions), and cache hit rates. Can be serialized to JSON for monitoring integration.

set_user(user_id: str, role: str = 'analyst') → User[source]

Set current user for operations.

Parameters:

  • user_id – User identifier

  • role – User role (“admin”, “analyst”, “reader”, “guest”)

Returns:

User object

get_audit_log(buffer_id: str | None = None, since: str | None = None, operations: list[str] | None = None, limit: int = 100) → dict[str, Any][source]

Query the audit log for operations.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Filter by buffer ID.

  • since – ISO timestamp (e.g., “2026-05-01”).

  • operations – Filter by operation types.

  • limit – Max entries.

Returns:

Dict with entries list.

get_test_context(buffer_id: str, file: str, top_k: int = 10) → dict[str, Any][source]

Find test files that reference a given source file.

Uses file naming heuristics and embedding similarity to find test files related to the source file.

Returns:

Dict with test_files list.

suggest_tests(buffer_id: str, changed_files: list[str], top_k: int = 10) → dict[str, Any][source]

Suggest tests to run after file changes.

Parameters:

  • buffer_id – Buffer handle.

  • changed_files – List of changed file paths.

  • top_k – Max suggestions.

Returns:

Dict with suggested_tests list.

get_audit_stats() → dict[str, Any][source]

Get audit log statistics.

Returns:

Statistics dictionary

trace_call_chain(buffer_id: str, from_symbol: str, to_symbol: str, max_depth: int = 10) → dict[str, Any][source]

Find call chain between two symbols using BFS.

get_dependencies(buffer_id: str, file: str, direction: str = 'both') → dict[str, Any][source]

Get file dependencies (outgoing imports, incoming references).

find_circular_dependencies(buffer_id: str) → dict[str, Any][source]

Find circular import/reference cycles in a buffer.

export_dependency_graph(buffer_id: str, format: str = 'json') → dict[str, Any][source]

Export dependency graph in JSON or DOT format.

find_dead_code(buffer_id: str, min_confidence: str = 'medium') → dict[str, Any][source]

Find dead code and unused symbols in a buffer.

extract_todos(buffer_id: str, tag: str | None = None) → dict[str, Any][source]

Extract TODO, FIXME, HACK, XXX comments from a buffer.

score_code_quality(buffer_id: str, file: str) → dict[str, Any][source]

Score code quality metrics for a file.

remember(key: str, value: str, tags: list[str] | None = None) → dict[str, Any][source]

Store a fact in conversation memory.

recall(query: str, top_k: int = 5) → dict[str, Any][source]

Recall facts from conversation memory by query.

list_memories(tag: str | None = None) → dict[str, Any][source]

List all stored memories, optionally filtered by tag.

forget(key: str) → dict[str, Any][source]

Remove a stored memory.

close() → None[source]

Release all in-memory resources.

Clears the FAISS index cache (drops GPU VRAM), the lexical index cache, and the query result cache. The on-disk registry and buffer files are left intact — they can be reloaded by constructing a new CodeEmbeddingTool with the same work_dir.

Also stops the Prometheus metrics HTTP server if enabled.

    • See Also

Utilities

Quick Reference

Most Used Classes:

  • gigacode.gigacode_tool.CodeEmbeddingTool - Main entry point

  • gigacode.index_manager.IndexManager - Index management

  • gigacode.search_service.SearchService - Search operations

  • gigacode.buffer_manager.BufferManager - Buffer management

Most Used Methods:

  • CodeEmbeddingTool.embed_codebase() - Embed a project

  • CodeEmbeddingTool.semantic_search() - Search by meaning

  • CodeEmbeddingTool.hybrid_search() - Semantic + lexical search

  • CodeEmbeddingTool.write_code() - Update code

  • CodeEmbeddingTool.commit() - Commit changes

  • CodeEmbeddingTool.find_duplicates() - Find similar code

Configuration:

  • See gigacode.operation_config.OperationConfig for all settings

  • Defaults suitable for most projects

  • Tune for your specific needs

See Also

  • Basic Embedding Workflow - Embedding tutorial

  • Search Workflows - Search examples

  • Edit Workflows - Editing tutorial

  • Performance Tuning - Optimization guide
